originalTitle: Anthropic is paying $15 billion a year for access to Elon Musk’s data centers
summary: Earlier this month, SpaceX and Anthropic announced a new compute partnership that provides access to the rocket company's Colossus data centers in Memphis, TN. Now, with the release of SpaceX's IPO filing, we have more details about that deal, including how much Anthropic is paying to Elon Musk's company. In its S-1 filing, SpaceX said […]
content: Earlier this month, SpaceX and Anthropic announced a new compute partnership that provides access to the rocket company’s Colossus data centers in Memphis, TN. Now, with the release of SpaceX’s IPO filing, we have more details about that deal, including how much Anthropic is paying to Elon Musk’s company. In its S-1 filing, SpaceX said that Anthropic agreed to pay $1.25 billion per month through May 2029 for access to SpaceX’s AI training centers at Colossus I and Colossus II. That’s $15 billion annually, or nearly double the $18.7 billion in revenue that SpaceX reported in all of 2025. The agreement includes a clause in which either company can terminate the deal within 90 days. And Anthropic’s fees will be reduced during the capacity ramp-up this month and next. The exit clause was likely necessary based on the fast-moving nature of the AI industry. In some sense, Anthropic’s Claude competes with X’s Grok. And it’s a sign of how desperate AI companies like Anthropic are for compute capacity, especially as the data center build out across the country runs into serious local opposition. In a post on X, Musk said that SpaceX stands ready to offer similar deals to other AI companies that want to access the data centers. SpaceX is “offering AI compute as a service at significant scale,” he said. SpaceX has been spending enormous amounts of money on AI since the company merged with Elon Musk’s xAI earlier this year. According to the filing, the rocket company spent $12.7 billion in capital expenditures on AI in 2025, or about 61 percent of the total spend. It spent $7.7 billion in the first quarter of 2026, compared to just $1 billion on its space division. SpaceX’s AI division lost $6.3 billion in operations on $3.2 billion in revenue in 2025, and lost $2.5 billion on $818 million in revenue in the first quarter of 2026. Meanwhile, Anthropic is cruising toward its first quarterly operating profit, with sales revenue expected reach at least $10.9 billion, more than double its $4.8 ⁠billion in revenue for the just-ended March quarter, Reuters reports.
chineseTitle: Anthropic 每年支付 150 亿美元用于访问埃隆·马斯克的数据中心
chineseSummary: 本月早些时候，SpaceX 和 Anthropic 宣布建立新的计算合作伙伴关系，提供对火箭公司位于田纳西州孟菲斯的 Colossus 数据中心的访问。现在，随着 SpaceX IPO 文件的发布，我们获得了有关该交易的更多细节，包括 Anthropic 向埃隆·马斯克的公司支付了多少钱。 SpaceX 在其 S-1 文件中表示 [...]
chineseContent: 本月早些时候，SpaceX 和 Anthropic 宣布建立新的计算合作伙伴关系，提供对火箭公司位于田纳西州孟菲斯的 Colossus 数据中心的访问。现在，随着 SpaceX IPO 文件的发布，我们获得了有关该交易的更多细节，包括 Anthropic 向埃隆·马斯克的公司支付了多少钱。 SpaceX 在其 S-1 文件中表示，Anthropic 同意在 2029 年 5 月之前每月支付 12.5 亿美元，用于使用 SpaceX 在 Colossus I 和 Colossus II 的人工智能培训中心。这相当于每年 150 亿美元，几乎是 SpaceX 报告的 2025 年全年收入 187 亿美元的两倍。该协议包括一项条款，任何一家公司都可以在 90 天内终止交易。 Anthropic 的费用将在本月和下个月的产能提升期间降低。鉴于人工智能行业快速发展的性质，退出条款可能是必要的。从某种意义上说，Anthropic 的 Claude 与 X 的 Grok 是竞争者。这表明像 Anthropic 这样的人工智能公司对计算能力的渴望是多么迫切，尤其是在全国范围内建设数据中心遇到当地严重反对的情况下。马斯克在 X 上的一篇帖子中表示，SpaceX 随时准备向其他想要访问数据中心的人工智能公司提供类似的交易。他表示，SpaceX 正在“大规模提供人工智能计算即服务”。自从今年早些时候与埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 的 xAI 合并以来，SpaceX 一直在人工智能方面投入了大量资金。文件显示，2025 年，这家火箭公司在人工智能上的资本支出为 127 亿美元，约占总支出的 61%。该公司在 2026 年第一季度的支出为 77 亿美元，而其太空部门的支出仅为 10 亿美元。 SpaceX 的人工智能部门 2025 年运营亏损 63 亿美元，营收 32 亿美元，亏损 25 亿美元，营收 818 美元
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
